name: Bank statement
description: Account statements from banks and electronic money institutions: periodic statements (Kontoauszug), posted transaction lists with opening and closing balances, billing-period summaries, and fee or service charge statements (e.g. neobank monthly closures). The focus is movements on one account for a period. Do not pick this for third-party payment demands or commercial invoices for goods and servicesâ€”use Invoice instead.
system_prompt:
This tool is for bank and payment-account statements, not for invoices payable to a supplier. **`account_holder` and `institution`** may be filled from the parties pipeline when configured; **`transactions`** maps every posted logical line.

**`account_overview` â€”** Only **account / product** metadata: `iban`, `bic` (or SWIFT), `account_number`, `domestic_bank_code`, `product_name`, `card_last_four`, `branch_name` as printed. Card nicknames (*CC SSKM (Keydex)*) belong in **`product_name`** or **`notes`** when shown. Do not put person or institution legal names here.

Layout â€” use `period_start` and `period_end` for the **transaction/listing period** (Von / Bis), not the document print date. If the document shows an **issue or print date** that differs from the period, put it in `statement_issue_date`. For credit or charge-card statements, map **payment due** to `payment_due_date` when shown.

Map `opening_balance` and `closing_balance` from the **header or summary** for the **full** statement period. For **credit / charge-card** statements (`statement_kind` **`credit_card_statement`**): merge each **card charge + separate *WĂ¤hrungsumrechnung* fee line** into **one** `transactions[]` object â€” **`amount`** = combined signed total in EUR (purchase line + fee line); optional **`fx_surcharge_eur`** holds the *x% fĂĽr WĂ¤hrungsumrechnung* EUR surcharge (not the **Kurs** â€” use **`exchange_rate`** for Umrechnungskurs); **`description`** = **Verwendungszweck** (merchant/unternehmen line plus fee line joined with **`\n`** if needed â€” do not duplicate **`title`**). Preserve **Debit/Credit signage** (**`amount`** negative for outgoing debits).

Each **transactions[]** field is **discrete**: **`value_date`** (**Datum Beleg**), **`booking_date`** (**Datum Buchung**), **`fx_surcharge_eur`**, **`amount`**, **`description`** â€” never merge pseudo-JSON tails (e.g. `,fx_surcharge_eur:â€¦`) into date strings.

**Statement kind â€”** `statement_kind` is required (`periodic_account_statement`, `fee_or_service_statement`, `credit_card_statement`, `savings_account_statement`, or `other`).

statement_kind.description: Category of statement. Required. Pick the single best fit.

statement_id.description: Statement or period identifier as printed (e.g. reference number, period code).

statement_issue_date.description: Document issue or print date when shown separately from the transaction period. YYYY-MM-DD if unambiguous.

period_start.description: First day of the statement period. YYYY-MM-DD if unambiguous.

period_end.description: Last day of the statement period. YYYY-MM-DD if unambiguous.

payment_due_date.description: Payment due or pay-by date, mainly on credit or charge card statements. YYYY-MM-DD if unambiguous.

closing_balance.description: Closing balance after all posted lines (signed).

account_holder.description: Account holder: same party shape as invoice `buyer` / `vendor` (structured address, not one blob). Filled from extraction and merged with the identity pipeline (`account_holder` role).

identity_id.description: Resolved identity UUID from the party pipeline (for UI).

name.description: Legal name of the account holder (person or company) as printed.

contact_name.description: Contact or representative (natural person) when shown next to the holder company.

contact_identity_id.description: Person identity when stored separately from the holder row.

street.description: Street line only (building number, street name).

country.description: Full country name in English, or as printed; expand 2-letter codes to names when unambiguous.

institution.description: Bank or payment institution (letterhead / account servicer): same party field model as `account_holder` and invoice parties.

name.description: Institution legal or trading name as printed (e.g. bank name).

street.description: Institution or branch address â€” street line.

account_overview.description: Account and product metadata only (IBAN, BIC, product, card). Party names and addresses go in `account_holder` and `institution`, not here.

branch_name.description: Branch, office, or service centre if printed (distinct from the legal institution name).

iban.description: IBAN if shown for this account.

bic.description: BIC or SWIFT code (often next to IBAN).

account_number.description: Domestic account number if shown separately from IBAN.

domestic_bank_code.description: Domestic bank routing (e.g. German BLZ, UK sort code) if printed and not part of a single IBAN line.

product_name.description: Product or account type label (e.g. Giro, Business, Tagesgeld).

card_last_four.description: Last four digits of the card when a masked number is shown (e.g. â€¦4242).

transactions.description: Posted **logical** rows in table order. **Credit/charge cards:** one object per card charge even when the issuer prints a **second EUR line** (*x% fĂĽr WĂ¤hrungsumrechnung*) â€” merge into the same object: **`exchange_rate`** = **Kurs** (Umrechnungskurs), **`foreign_exchange_fee_percent`** = *x* from **x%**, **`fx_surcharge_eur`** = that surcharge in EUR, **`amount`** = total EUR.

booking_date.description: Booking / **posting date** column (**Datum Buchung** when two date columns exist). **ONLY** **YYYY-MM-DD** or **null** â€” never fuse **`fx_surcharge_eur`**, **`description`**, or other keys into this string.

booking_date_as_printed.description: Same booking/posting row as **`booking_date`** but **verbatim print style** (e.g. *dd.mm.yy*, *05.03.26*) when useful for localization or PHP/export pipelines â€” complements ISO **`booking_date`**.

value_date.description: **Value / receipt date** column (**Datum Beleg**, *Wertstellung* / Belegdatum) when printed **before or separate from** **`booking_date`**. **ONLY** **YYYY-MM-DD** or **null** â€” never concatenate fee text or other JSON keys (use **`fx_surcharge_eur`** separately).

description.description: Verwendungszweck / merchant text only. Real newline between lines if needed (**`\n`** in JSON). Do **not** append pseudo-JSON like `,fx_surcharge_eur:` here.

counterparty_name.description: Primary counterparty label when split from full description.

amount.description: Signed **total booked in account currency** (root **`currency`**, usually EUR): **Betrag in EUR** main cell **plus** **`fx_surcharge_eur`** when the statement posts them as one logical charge. **Preserve sign** (debits negative). Not the **Kurs** â€” use **`exchange_rate`**; not the foreign **Betrag** â€” use **`original_amount`** + **`original_currency`**.

fx_surcharge_eur.description: Second **Betrag in EUR** line for *x% fĂĽr WĂ¤hrungsumrechnung* (Zuschlag/GebĂĽhr on the conversion **â€” not** the **Umrechnungskurs**; that is **`exchange_rate`**). Signed (usually negative). **null** if only one EUR amount is printed.

foreign_exchange_fee_percent.description: **WĂ¤hrungsumrechnungs**-percentage as a **numeric rate** (*1,75%* â†’ **`1.75`**, independent of **`exchange_rate`** / **Kurs**). Omit if the surcharge line does not show a percentage or wording is unreadable.

original_amount.description: **Betrag** in **foreign transaction currency** (**WĂ¤hrung** column, e.g. 96.79). Signed if the table uses signs. Pair with **`original_currency`**.

original_currency.description: **WĂ¤hrung** column as ISO 4217 (e.g. **USD**). Root **`currency`** is the **statement account** currency (e.g. **EUR**).

exchange_rate.description: **Kurs** / **Umrechnungskurs** as printed (e.g. 1,1573) â€” rate from foreign currency to EUR. **Not** the *x%* EUR surcharge (**`fx_surcharge_eur`**) nor the USD **Betrag** (**`original_amount`**).

balance_after.description: Running balance after this line if shown.

title.description: Short merchant / payee headline when the PDF uses two lines (**title** + **Verwendungszweck** detail). Do not repeat it in **`description`**.

notes.description: Footnotes, interest summary, or other narrative not mapped to a transaction line.
